project.name: GroundAtlas
project.summary: Open-source CLI-first knowledge control plane that generates source-grounded repository maps for humans and AI agents without becoming a second source of truth.
goals: Generate deterministic source-grounded maps over repository truth surfaces.
Help humans and AI agents enter repositories faster while preserving canonical source ownership.
Provide safe CLI operations for init, update, scan, audit, explain, and impact analysis.
Expose machine-readable outputs suitable for CI gates and future agent/MCP integrations.
Expose typed library exports for scanner, audit, explain, impact, and rendering primitives.
Publish the package to npm through CI-owned provenance and registry readback.
Dogfood GroundAtlas on its own repository in every required check.
nonGoals: Own or replace downstream project truth such as ADRs, schemas, tests, manifests, workflows, or source code.
Mutate source files or root agent prompts in scanned repositories.
Require LLM/provider credentials, hosted tracing, or remote state in the MVP.
Publish packages manually from a workstation.

owns.name: groundatlas-cli
owns.description: CLI commands and deterministic scanner/renderer behavior for source-grounded repository maps.

owns.name: generated-map-format
owns.description: Machine-readable atlas JSON and generated Markdown navigation files.

owns.name: groundatlas-audit-gate
owns.description: Local audit rules that verify generated maps exist and declare their non-SSOT boundary.

doesNotOwn: Downstream repository architecture, API, persistence, deployment, or release decisions.
Enterprise doctrine rules owned by SylphxAI/doctrine.
Package-registry publication until release automation is explicitly added.

allowedDependencies.surface: engineering doctrine, project manifest schema, and standards routing

forbiddenCouplings: Generated maps must never become canonical project truth.
GroundAtlas must not require private Sylphx infrastructure to run as an open-source CLI.
GroundAtlas must not mutate scanned source files or root agent prompts.
Future LLM integrations must remain optional adapters over deterministic source manifests.

delivery.deployPath: Source changes are delivered by GitHub PR/CI. npm publication is prepared through .github/workflows/release.yml and requires npm trusted publishing or an npm identity/token before first registry publish.
delivery.productionProof: Clean checkout runs bun install and bun run check successfully; package release proof requires npm registry readback for groundatlas.

gaps.description: Create the first architecture ADR through the PR-number-based ADR workflow once the repository has a normal PR path.
Configure npm trusted publishing or a bounded one-time npm token, then publish and read back groundatlas from npm.
gaps.target: Before claiming npm package publication complete

gaps.description: After first publish, verify npm view groundatlas and smoke install the package in a clean temp project.
